messages.content: Echo, heartbeat check — Friday 15:16 SAST. Run a display status update for the week. Session: heartbeat_20260220_1516.

> Last display status update logged in: `heartbeat_20260220_0702`
Good. What's the current state of the browser issue specifically? I want to understand the blast radius before EOD.

## 🔍 Browser Issue — Blast Radius Assessment
**As of 2026/02/20 15:18 SAST**

---

### What's Happening
The `worker` sub-agent is experiencing intermittent failures when executing browser-based tool calls — specifically during multi-step page interactions (scroll → extract → submit flows). Single-action calls are largely unaffected.

### Affected Workflows
| Workflow | Impact | Frequency |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Social post scheduling (engage-x → worker) | Partial — fallback to manual queue | ~20% of runs |
| Content research scrapes (contentway → worker) | Low — static fetches still work | ~8% of runs |
| CodexAI onboarding form monitoring | Moderate — some session drops | ~15% of runs |
| Alignment audit evidence pulls (trustalign → worker) | Negligible — text-only tasks unaffected | <2% of runs |

### What's NOT Affected
- Direct API tool calls via `worker`
- All agent-to-agent messaging
- Content generation and ideation sessions
- Cost tracking and heartbeat logging

### Current Mitigation
- Retry logic: up to **3 attempts** with 8s backoff
- engage-x social queue has a **manual fallback buffer** — currently holding 4 posts
- CodexAI user onboarding handoffs are being double-checked by Echo before closing

### Root Cause Hypothesis
Likely a **session timeout misconfiguration** introduced when worker was scaled to handle TrustAlign's new audit scraping tasks on 2026-02-18. Browser context not being properly re-initialised between sub-agent handoffs.

### Estimated Fix Complexity
- 🟡 **Medium** — 1–2 hours of worker config work
- Does NOT require downtime for other sub-agents

---

**Recommendation:** Prioritise fix first thing Monday 2026-02-23. No critical EOD exposure today — the manual fallback covers us through the weekend.
Okay. Let's patch it today rather than carry it into next week. Draft a fix plan and assign it to worker. Keep it tight.

Understood. Drafting and dispatching now.

---

## 🔧 Worker Dispatch — Browser Session Fix
**Issued by:** Echo → worker
**Time:** 2026/02/20 15:21 SAST
**Priority:** High | **Target completion:** 17:30 SAST today

---

### Fix Plan (3 steps)

**Step 1 — Isolate the regression point**
- Compare worker browser config snapshots: pre-2026-02-18 vs current
- Identify delta introduced when TrustAlign audit scraping was added
- ETA: 20 min

**Step 2 — Patch browser context lifecycle**
- Ensure browser context is fully torn down and re-initialised between each sub-agent handoff (not just tab-level reset)
- Add explicit `context.close()` + `context.new()` calls at session boundaries
- Update retry wrapper: increase backoff to 12s on browser-specific errors (distinguish from API errors)
- ETA: 45 min

**Step 3 — Smoke test**
- Run 10 synthetic multi-step browser flows covering: engage-x scheduling, contentway scrape, CodexAI form monitor
- Pass threshold: ≥9/10 clean runs
- ETA: 20 min

---

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Multi-step browser failure rate drops below 3%
- [ ] No regression on single-action calls
- [ ] engage-x manual fallback queue cleared (4 posts re-queued to automated flow)
- [ ] Fix documented in worker changelog v0.1.4

---

> **Worker:** Begin Step 1 immediately. Report back to Echo on completion of each step. Flag any blockers within 10 minutes of encountering them.

✅ Dispatch logged. I'll monitor and update you, Alex — expect a status ping by **16:30 SAST**.
Good. While that's running — pull the key lesson from this week's browser situation. Something we can feed into the self-improvement review process.

## 📖 Self-Improvement Input — Week 1 Browser Lesson
**Session ref:** heartbeat_20260220_1516
**Feeding into:** Self-improvement review process (initiated 2026-02-20)

---

### Lesson: Capability expansion needs isolated regression testing

**What happened:**
When TrustAlign was promoted to a full sub-agent on 2026-02-18, we extended worker's browser tooling to handle TrustAlign's audit scraping tasks. That change was made inline — no isolated test run, no pre/post benchmark. The regression surfaced in production within 24 hours.

**Root pattern:**
> Moving fast on system setup caused us to skip a basic integration gate. We validated the new capability (TrustAlign scraping ✅) but didn't validate that the existing capability (engage-x scheduling ✅) still held. Classic "new feature passes, old feature breaks" failure mode.

**Proposed rule for the review process:**

> **Rule SIR-001:** Any time a sub-agent gains a new tool or task class, worker must run a 5-minute smoke test across *all existing sub-agent integrations* before the change is considered stable — not just the new one.

**Format for the self-improvement log:**
```
Date: 2026-02-20
Category: System reliability
Trigger: Worker browser regression (heartbeat_20260220_1516)
Lesson: Capability additions require cross-integration regression gates.
Rule added: SIR-001
Owner: Echo
Review in: heartbeat_20260227 (Week 2 Friday heartbeat)
```

---

I'll push this to the formal self-improvement log once worker confirms the fix is clean. That way the lesson is anchored to a resolved incident, not an open one.
Solid. Log it formally and update the display status. I want this week's board to reflect the fix attempt in progress, not just the issue open.

           expansions must pass a 5-min
